node.title: Uber | Phone | Games Giant vertical game

topic.title: Uber | Phone | Games Giant vertical game

post.content: Tried to write clean code.\n<iframe src="https://leetcode.com/playground/XLYSiSgK/shared" frameBorder="0" width="1000" height="600"></iframe>

Like Tic Tac Toe... the only difference being, initialize an array last_filled_row[col], that keeps the index of the last_filled_row[col] (initialize all of them to rows-1). so you can do board insertions in O(1) time, by simply looking up the last_filled_row[col] that the piece should slide down to.\n\nThis in combination with the auxiliary arrays (like in Tic Tac Toe) to quickly check for 4 in each of: row/col/diag/antidiag, and you can achieve overall O(1) time complexity for this move function.
Sounds like the American (Western?) game called "Connect 4".  Just like Tic-Tac-Toe, certain moves will always lead to a win.

The function "Result move (player p, int col)" is used to determine the outcome of a move made by a player in the game. The input parameters of the function are the player (p) who made the move and the column (col) in which the move was made.

The function first checks if the selected column is already full. If it is, the function returns "invalid move" as the outcome.

Otherwise, the function drops the coin of the current player in the bottom-most empty cell of the selected column. It then checks if the current player has won by having four coins in a horizontal, vertical, diagonal or anti-diagonal line. If so, the function returns "player p wins" as the outcome.

If the current player has not won, the function checks if the board is full. If it is, the function returns "tie" as the outcome.

Otherwise, the function returns "continue" as the outcome, indicating that the game is still in progress and the next player can make a move. find mire here https://namecombinertool.com/
